       arXiv:2308.14132  (cs)
     
 
   
     
  [Submitted on 27 Aug 2023 ( v1 ), last revised 7 Nov 2023 (this version, v3)] 
     Title: Detecting Language Model Attacks with Perplexity 
     Authors: Gabriel Alon ,  Michael Kamfonas              View a PDF of the paper titled Detecting Language Model Attacks with Perplexity, by Gabriel Alon and 1 other authors 
     View PDF 



     
             Abstract: A novel hack involving Large Language Models (LLMs) has emerged, exploiting adversarial suffixes to deceive models into generating perilous responses. Such jailbreaks can trick LLMs into providing intricate instructions to a malicious user for creating explosives, orchestrating a bank heist, or facilitating the creation of offensive content. By evaluating the perplexity of queries with adversarial suffixes
